status_notes: Initial research from GCP GKE documentation, 2026-04-04. Needs cross-reference pass and human review with live GCP environment.
notes: Managed Kubernetes for GCP. Security focus: workload identity, binary authorization, network policies, private cluster, shielded nodes.

match_nodes.description: Map Kubernetes service accounts to Google IAM service accounts

data.description: Workload Identity is the recommended way for GKE workloads to access Google Cloud APIs. It federates Kubernetes service accounts with Google IAM service accounts. Pods authenticate as the mapped IAM service account without using static service account keys or node-level credentials.
data.vendor_recommendation: Always use Workload Identity. Never use node service account credentials or download service account keys. Each application should have its own dedicated IAM service account with minimal permissions.

audit.note: Check service account annotations: kubectl get sa {sa} -n {ns} -o yaml | grep iam.gke.io/gcp-service-account

match_nodes.description: Enforce container image signing policies before deployment

data.description: Binary Authorization ensures only trusted container images are deployed to GKE. Define policies that require images to be signed by trusted authorities (attestors) before they can run. Integrates with Cloud Build for automated attestation in CI/CD pipelines.
data.vendor_recommendation: Enable Binary Authorization for production clusters. Require attestations from your CI/CD pipeline. Use break-glass procedures for emergencies.

configure.cli_policy: gcloud container binauthz policy import policy.yaml

match_nodes.description: Pod-to-pod traffic filtering via Calico or Dataplane V2

data.description: GKE network policies control pod-to-pod communication using Kubernetes NetworkPolicy resources. Implemented via Calico (standard) or GKE Dataplane V2 (eBPF-based, newer). Without network policies, all pods in the cluster can communicate freely.

options.name: Calico
options.description: Standard network policy enforcement. Must be enabled at cluster creation.

options.name: Dataplane V2
options.description: eBPF-based. Built-in network policy support. Better performance and observability.

data.vendor_recommendation: Enable network policies for all production clusters. Use Dataplane V2 for new clusters. Start with deny-all default, then allow specific traffic.

configure.terraform: network_policy block or datapath_provider on google_container_cluster

match_nodes.description: Nodes with internal IPs only — API server private or authorized networks

data.description: Private GKE clusters give nodes only internal IP addresses. The control plane (API server) can be private or public with authorized networks. Private nodes cannot reach the internet without Cloud NAT. Reduces attack surface by eliminating public node IPs.

options.name: Public cluster
options.description: Default. Nodes have external IPs. API server is public.

options.name: Private nodes
options.description: Nodes have internal IPs only. API server can be public or private.

options.name: Private endpoint
options.description: API server only accessible via internal IP. Most restrictive.

data.vendor_recommendation: Use private clusters for production. Enable authorized networks to restrict API server access. Use Cloud NAT for node outbound internet access.

match_nodes.description: Shielded VM for GKE nodes — secure boot + integrity monitoring

data.description: Shielded GKE nodes use Shielded VM features (secure boot, vTPM, integrity monitoring) for the underlying node VMs. Protects the node boot process from tampering, rootkits, and bootkits. Enabled by default for new clusters.

default.note: Shielded nodes enabled by default for new GKE clusters.

data.vendor_recommendation: Enable shielded nodes for all clusters. Do not disable. Verify integrity monitoring alerts are routed to your security team.

match_nodes.description: Encrypt Kubernetes secrets at rest with Cloud KMS

data.description: Application-layer secrets encryption uses a Cloud KMS key to encrypt Kubernetes secrets at rest in the etcd datastore. Without this, secrets are encrypted at the storage layer by Google but not at the application layer — the GKE control plane can read them.
data.vendor_recommendation: Enable application-layer secrets encryption for clusters handling sensitive data. Use a dedicated KMS key. Monitor key access in Cloud Audit Logs.

match_nodes.description: Restrict API server access to specific CIDRs

data.description: Master authorized networks restrict which IP address ranges can reach the GKE cluster API server. Acts as an IP-based allowlist for the Kubernetes API. Without this, the API server is accessible from any IP (if public).
data.vendor_recommendation: Enable authorized networks for all clusters with public endpoints. Allowlist only known admin networks, CI/CD systems, and VPN egress IPs.

match_nodes.description: GKE Security Posture — vulnerability and misconfiguration scanning

data.description: GKE Security Posture provides a dashboard for security insights across your GKE fleet. Scans workloads for vulnerabilities in container images, Kubernetes misconfigurations (pod security, RBAC), and compliance issues. Integrates with Security Command Center.

options.description: Scans running container images for known CVEs. Continuous monitoring.
Checks Kubernetes workload configurations against best practices and CIS benchmarks.
Correlates GKE security bulletins with your cluster versions.

data.vendor_recommendation: Enable security posture for all clusters. Review findings regularly. Prioritize critical vulnerabilities in running workloads.

configure.cli: gcloud container clusters update {cluster} --security-posture standard --workload-vulnerability-scanning standard

match_nodes.description: Container image vulnerability scanning in Artifact Registry

data.description: Artifact Registry integrates with Container Analysis to provide automatic vulnerability scanning of container images. Scans detect known CVEs in OS packages and language packages. Supports on-push scanning and continuous monitoring of deployed images.
data.vendor_recommendation: Enable vulnerability scanning for all container repositories. Block deployment of images with critical/high vulnerabilities using Binary Authorization. Review scan results before promoting images to production.

configure.note: Scanning is automatic for Artifact Registry once the Container Scanning API is enabled. No per-repo configuration needed.
